title: Hermannia modesta
taxonomy: Hermannia modesta is a member of the Malvaceae family, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as mallows. This family is part of the larger order Malvales, which also includes other flowering plant families such as the Bixaceae and the Cephalotaceae. The Malvaceae family is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world, including Africa, Asia, Australia, and the Americas. Hermannia modesta is a specific species within the Hermannia genus, which contains approximately 60 species of flowering plants. The classification of Hermannia modesta reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants and its unique characteristics.

intro: Hermannia modesta, also known as the modest hermannia or modest round-leaved hermannia, is a plant species in the family Malvaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Hermannia modesta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Hermannia modesta is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are employed to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
